title: Tempting Smoky Ethiopian Soybean Sprouts Doro Wot
description: A smoky and romantic Ethiopian dish featuring soybean sprouts, cooked in a clay pot and perfect for a Buddhist temple friendly meal. This cheese-pull doro wot is a unique twist on traditional Ethiopian cuisine.
instructions: Heat the clay pot over medium heat and add the vegetable oil.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Add the berbere spice and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the soybean sprouts and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the tomato paste and water, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes.
Season the doro wot with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve the doro wot hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
